metrics.bodyTextSample: AI Memory needs the server The app could not reach your AI Memory server. This fallback page can load from the device cache, but Ask, capture, export, and sync require server access. There is no offline queue in UX v2. If this is a new device, open AI Memory online once so the fallback page can be cached. Connected (26 ms). Redirecting… Retry Library Pair device If retry keeps failing: check your internet connection, wait a moment, and try again. If the issue persists, the AI Memory server may be down. /* * This inline script intentionally does NOT import reachability.ts — * the offline page is served from /public and must render even when * the Next.js server is dead, so bundled ES modules are not available. * Keep this in sync with src/lib/client/reachability.ts at the wire * level: 2s timeout, GET /api/health, cache-bypass, status-code case * analysis mirrors the TS module. * * Why no bearer token: the offline page runs before pairing is * confirmed and cannot reliably read Capacitor Preferences via * bundled plugin imports from a static /public page. We probe the * current origin without auth — the proxy responds 401 if the * server is up but the token path is wanted, which is enough signal * to distinguish "AI Memory up" from "AI Memory down / unreachable". The * richer bearer-authed probe runs from share-handler.tsx using the * canonical reachability.ts module. */ (function () { const statusEl = document.getElementById("status"); const retryBtn = document.getElementById
metrics.title: AI Memory — offline
